records.ety: [L. facilis, prop., capable of being done or made, hence, facile, easy, fr. facere to make, do: cf. F. facile. Srr Fact, and cf. Faculty.]

defs: Easy to be done or performed: not difficult; performable or attainable with little labor.
quotes: Order . . . will render the work facile and delightful.

defs: Easy to be surmounted or removed; easily conquerable; readily mastered.
quotes: The facile gates of hell too slightly barred.

defs: Easy of access or converse; mild; courteous; not haughty, austere, or distant; affable; complaisant.
quotes: I meant she should be courteous, facile, sweet.

defs: Easily persuaded to good or bad; yielding; ductile to a fault; pliant; flexible.
quotes: Since Adam, and his facile consort Eve, Lost Paradise, deceived by me.
This is treating Burns like a child, a person of so facile a disposition as not to be trusted without a keeper on the king's highway.

defs: Ready; quick; expert; as, he is facile in expedients; he wields a facile pen.
